---
title: "PLACEHOLDER: Introduction to Systematic Literature Reviews"
description: "An overview of what a systematic literature review is, how it differs from a narrative review, and how to plan and execute one."
author: "Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch"
date: 2026-06-15
date-modified: 2026-06-15
image: ../../assets/images/placeholder.png

learning-objectives:
  - "Distinguish a systematic literature review from a narrative or scoping review"
  - "Identify the key stages of a systematic review and their purpose"
  - "Formulate a clear research question suitable for a systematic review"

categories:
  - literature-review
  - BA
  - MA
  - reading

level:
  - BA
  - MA

content-type: reading

citation:
  type: entry
  container-title: "Methods Hub"
  title: "Introduction to Systematic Literature Reviews"
  editor:
    - name: "Claudius Gräbner-Radkowitsch"
  url: https://euf-methodology-hub.netlify.app/resources/literature-review/intro-to-systematic-literature-review
---

# What is a Systematic Literature Review?

*Placeholder content — this page is a structural scaffold for the Methods Hub Phase 1 build. Full content will be written in a subsequent phase.*

A systematic literature review follows a pre-specified, reproducible protocol for searching, selecting, and synthesizing existing research on a topic. Unlike a narrative review, the process is documented in enough detail that another researcher could replicate the search and arrive at the same pool of studies.

# Key Stages

*Content to be added.*

# Formulating a Research Question

*Content to be added.*

::: {.callout-note .level-advanced}
## MA / PhD

At the MA and PhD level, a systematic review typically also requires pre-registration of the review protocol (e.g., on PROSPERO or OSF), a formal PRISMA flow diagram, and a risk-of-bias assessment for included studies. These requirements signal that the review is intended to be a primary contribution to the literature, not merely a summary for a thesis background chapter.

*Placeholder — MA/PhD extension content to be added.*
:::

# Practical Tools

*Content to be added.*
